title: [GENERAL]
text: [GENERAL] The [GENERAL] section contains configuration settings for compression and encoding of binary data, such as images. Configuration file: psdfef.txt (Unix only) Explain this code [GENERAL] compression: flate | rle | none encoding: asc85 | hex Copy Arguments Setting Value Meaning compression rle Run Length Encoding (RLE) compression algorithm. The resulting compressed binary data is not larger than the original binary data. This is suitable for 2-color images (black and white, no gray-scale), such as line art. flate Compression algorithm based on the open-source data compression library zlib . It requires a PostScript Level 3 printer. This is suitable for most full-color or gray-scale images. none No compression (default) encoding asc85 ASCII85 encoding. 4 bytes of binary data is encoded to 5 ASCII characters. resulting in a 25% increase in the output PostScript file. It requires PostScript level 2 or above. This encoding is widely supported by printer manufacturers. hex 2-byte encoding. 1 byte of binary data is encoded to 2 ASCII characters, resulting in the a 100% increase in the output PostScript file. Because hex encoding was supported in the original PostScript specification, it is a fallback option if encoding is not specified. For example: [GENERAL] #compression: rle #compression: flate compression: none #encoding: hex encoding: asc85 Copy Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
